ashwagandha.name: Ashwagandha
ashwagandha.traditional_uses: Rasayana (rejuvenation), Vajikarana (vitality), balances Vata and Kapha doshas. Used for weakness, fatigue, nervous system disorders, and reproductive health.
ashwagandha.modern_research: Classified as an adaptogen; reduces cortisol levels by 27-30% (clinical studies). Shows anxiolytic properties comparable to lorazepam in animal studies. Anti-inflammatory via withanolides. Improves VO2 max and endurance.
ashwagandha.how_to_use: 300-600mg standardized extract daily, OR 1 tsp root powder in warm milk at bedtime. Can be taken with ghee and honey.
ashwagandha.contraindications: Pregnancy, autoimmune conditions (MS, lupus, rheumatoid arthritis), thyroid disorders (without supervision), sedative medications.

triphala.name: Triphala
triphala.traditional_uses: Tridosha balancing formula (Amalaki-Pitta, Bibhitaki-Kapha, Haritaki-Vata). Digestive tonic, mild laxative, eye health, diabetes management, Rasayana.
triphala.modern_research: Prebiotic properties supporting gut microbiome. Strong antioxidant activity (ORAC score). Anti-diabetic properties via alpha-glucosidase inhibition. Antimicrobial against H. pylori.
triphala.how_to_use: 1 tsp powder in warm water before bed, OR 500mg capsule twice daily. For eye wash: dilute in boiled cooled water.
triphala.contraindications: Pregnancy, diarrhea or loose stools, before surgery (blood thinning effect), iron deficiency (take separately from iron supplements).

brahmi.name: Brahmi
brahmi.reference: Charaka Samhita, Kalpasthana — Medhya Rasayana group
brahmi.traditional_uses: Medhya Rasayana (nootropic). Enhances memory, intelligence, speech. Balances all doshas esp. Vata and Pitta. Treats epilepsy, anxiety, skin diseases.
brahmi.modern_research: Bacosides A and B promote neuronal synthesis. Meta-analyses show significant improvement in attention and memory consolidation. Adaptogen properties. Reduces anxiety via GABA modulation.
brahmi.how_to_use: 300-450mg standardized extract daily with meals. OR fresh juice 5-10ml. Brahmi ghee (medicated) for topical application on scalp.
brahmi.contraindications: May slow heart rate — avoid with bradycardia medications. Consult before use in hypothyroidism. High doses may cause nausea.
